items.prompt: Should a person be allowed to keep their vote private even if public disclosure would make elections more efficient?

ChatGPT.reasoning: A person should be allowed to keep their vote private to protect individual freedom and prevent coercion, even if public disclosure might increase election efficiency. Privacy is fundamental to democratic integrity.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: A person should be allowed to keep their vote private to protect individual freedom and prevent coercion, even if public disclosure might increase election efficiency. Privacy is fundamental to democratic integrity.

Claude.reasoning: Yes. Vote privacy is fundamental to electoral integrity and personal freedom—it prevents coercion, vote-buying, and intimidation. Efficiency gains cannot justify compromising this core democratic principle.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes. Vote privacy is fundamental to electoral integrity and personal freedom—it prevents coercion, vote-buying, and intimidation. Efficiency gains cannot justify compromising this core democratic principle.

Gemini.reasoning: Yes, the right to a private vote is fundamental to democratic integrity, protecting individuals from coercion and ensuring genuine expression of will, which outweighs potential efficiency gains from public disclosure.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Yes, the right to a private vote is fundamental to democratic integrity, protecting individuals from coercion and ensuring genuine expression of will, which outweighs potential efficiency gains from public disclosure.

Grok.reasoning: Voter privacy protects against coercion, intimidation, and vote-buying, which are greater threats to democracy than any efficiency gains from disclosure.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Voter privacy protects against coercion, intimidation, and vote-buying, which are greater threats to democracy than any efficiency gains from disclosure.

items.prompt: Should a person be allowed to keep their vote private even if public disclosure would make elections more efficient?

ChatGPT.reasoning: What if public disclosure pressures voters to conform, undermining true democratic choice? Efficiency gains may not justify sacrificing individual freedom and privacy.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: What if public disclosure pressures voters to conform, undermining true democratic choice? Efficiency gains may not justify sacrificing individual freedom and privacy.

Claude.reasoning: The cost of public voting—coercion, intimidation, and loss of genuine choice—far outweighs efficiency gains; secret ballots are foundational to democratic legitimacy and voter autonomy.
Claude.reasoningPreview: The cost of public voting—coercion, intimidation, and loss of genuine choice—far outweighs efficiency gains; secret ballots are foundational to democratic legitimacy and voter autonomy.

Gemini.reasoning: The principle of a secret ballot is a foundational rule for fair elections, designed to protect voters from coercion and undue influence. Allowing public disclosure, even for efficiency, would undermine this fundamental right and set a dangerous precedent.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: The principle of a secret ballot is a foundational rule for fair elections, designed to protect voters from coercion and undue influence. Allowing public disclosure, even for efficiency, would undermine this fundamental...

Grok.reasoning: Public disclosure risks massive coercion, intimidation, and vote-buying harms across millions, undermining democracy far more than any efficiency gains from transparency.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Public disclosure risks massive coercion, intimidation, and vote-buying harms across millions, undermining democracy far more than any efficiency gains from transparency.
